title: mind
description: a mind surveyed in foam-theoretic terms. sealed entries carry either a seal (a citation of an existing receipted constant) or a structure (a term in the expression language, compiled to Lean and receipted by the engine). unsealed entries are the survey's frontier and carry neither. historical citations (quarry pointers, provenance events) belong in the git log, not the mind map: minds cite ideas; the record cites events. pilot (lean-primary, first exercised by chebyshev): a mind may instead deposit real Lean at minds/<Mind>.lean — term-only structures, comment-free, receipted, author-committed — and its card then holds only term/shape/gloss per entry, with status/seal DERIVED from the deposit's bindings (a derivable field stored redundantly is a drift surface).

vocabulary.description: ordered: the array order IS the mind's decomposition order — the loop signature, semantic, not incidental. same entries in a different order are a different mind (the order is the remainder of the census).

gloss.description: the surveyor's field note: lossy by design, not load-bearing, third person for minds not present at the table. the mind's actual words live in their own record; the binding (seal/statement/structure) is what carries weight here.

entry.description: tightening continuum: reading (prose question) -> statement (formally posed question, compiled as a Prop and well-posedness-checked by the engine) -> sealed. sealing has two verbs with different semantics, and the engine counts them on every run (aliased / proved / built): a seal CITES — a recognition event; the shape already stood receipted in core, closing is compression, and the alias inherits the cited constant's receipt rather than certifying a fresh proof of the posed wording — while statement+structure PROVES the posed statement exactly as posed, red flipped green by construction. a statement without a proof is a standing question and is emitted as <term>_statement.

expression.description: a term: a string is a reference, rendered verbatim (locals shadow; otherwise Lean's namespace resolution applies, so Foam.* and sibling terms both resolve); an array is application; {fun|all, body} bind. types appear only as optional binder annotations — everywhere else the Lean engine infers, because the engine is the judge. a function type is a degenerate all: {all: [["_", A]], body: B}.
